description_long: This lighthouse features a tall, cylindrical tower with a slightly tapered profile, constructed primarily from light-colored materials and reinforced with a visible framework of tan structural supports. The midsection is adorned with vertical window panels, allowing light to enter and providing visibility from within. A prominent gallery balcony encircles the upper portion, complete with a safety railing and accessible via a staircase that wraps around the lower section. The base is hexagonal and robust, supporting the structure and providing entry access through a visible door. The top of the lighthouse is capped with a lantern room, though the presence of a light beacon is not clearly visible, and the overall design suggests a blend of functionalist and early industrial architectural influences.
description: A tall, cylindrical lighthouse with a tan structural framework, gallery balcony, and hexagonal base, featuring vertical windows and a prominent upper platform.
description_view_0: The lighthouse is seen with its main staircase and entry door visible, showing the hexagonal base and the full wraparound balcony.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of the lighthouse, with the staircase and balcony extending outward, and a long horizontal support beam visible.
description_view_2: The entry door is clearly visible on the tower, with the balcony and structural supports framing the windows above.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the lighthouse is shown, with the balcony and horizontal support beam extending outward, and no entry door visible.
